reply:
(1) This device is an AutomationDirect GS10 Variable Frequency Drive. It controls motor speed by adjusting voltage and frequency.

(2) The most probable fault cause is wear on the motor bearings, as the last greasing was 6 months ago and the drive is likely part of the motor's control system.

(3) The tech should check the motor's bearing condition and replace them if worn. For the unknown fault, the tech should start by inspecting the drive's connections and settings, as it could be a cause of the motor's malfunction.
